responses.gist: School will close at 12:00 noon instead of the usual 3:30 PM. Students must leave the school building within 30 minutes after dismissal.
responses.whatThisMeans: Your child will finish school 3.5 hours earlier than usual. Pick-up time is now noon, not 3:30 PM.

suggestedActions.text: Consider arranging to pick up your child by 12:00 noon
You may want to inform anyone who usually picks up your child about the time change

responses.sourceQuote: 1. All classes from I to XII shall be dismissed at 12:00 noon instead of the regular dismissal time of 3:30 PM. Students are directed to vacate the school premises within 30 minutes of dismissal.

responses.gist: School buses will now leave at 12:30 PM instead of the regular time. Children using the bus must be at their bus assembly point by 12:15 PM. The school will NOT send separate messages to each parent about this.
responses.whatThisMeans: If your child takes the school bus, they need to be at their bus stop area by 12:15 PM. You will not get a personal phone call or message about this — this circular IS the notice.

suggestedActions.text: Consider telling your child the new bus time: be at assembly point by 12:15 PM
You may want to note: buses leave at 12:30 PM sharp — no waiting

responses.sourceQuote: 2. Parents/Guardians of students availing school bus transport are hereby informed that the bus departure schedule stands revised. Buses shall depart from the school campus at 12:30 PM sharp. Students must report to their respective bus assembly points by 12:15 PM. No separate intimation shall be sent to individual parents.

responses.gist: Teachers must keep drinking water available in classrooms. If any student shows signs of heat-related illness, it must be reported to the school medical room right away. Parents will be told within 24 hours if their child has heat exhaustion symptoms.
responses.whatThisMeans: The school is watching for heat illness. If your child feels dizzy, nauseous, or overheated, the school may contact you within a day.

suggestedActions.text: Consider making sure the school has your current phone number
You may want to teach your child to tell a teacher if they feel unwell from heat

responses.sourceQuote: 5. Teachers are directed to ensure adequate drinking water arrangements in their respective classrooms. Any case of heat-related illness must be reported to the school medical room immediately. Parents shall be contacted within 24 hours if their ward reports any symptoms of heat exhaustion.
